headline: Microsoft shifts OpenAI partnership into new revenue-sharing phase
body: Microsoft announced the next phase of its partnership with OpenAI, including changes to revenue sharing arrangements between the tech giants. This restructuring signals how enterprise AI deployment will be funded and controlled going forward, directly impacting healthcare systems evaluating AI vendor relationships. For health IT leaders, the partnership's evolution matters because it affects pricing models, integration pathways, and long-term viability of AI tools built on OpenAI's models. Healthcare organizations currently negotiating multi-year AI contracts should monitor these partnership terms closely, as they often cascade into clinical deployment costs and feature availability.
